rationale: Both candidates miss the highest-value confirmed GT bugs (stream-slot leak on abort, cumulative/delta chunk_np resplice, ceil-division/input_lengths mismatch, v2-tokenizer-swallows-legacy-checkpoint) — recall is low for both. X actually asserts the slot-leak is 'handled' via on_requests_finished, a false claim that directly contradicts the confirmed High-severity bug gcanlin fixed, which is a real precision ding, but X's own findings (max_new_frames guard removal, hardcoded talker_mtp sampling params, dead ref-codes output, v1/v2 codec load_weights fragility) are each grounded and specific with file/line. Y's findings are mostly duplicative 'no unit test' claims and it internally contradicts itself by citing 'modeling_moss_tts_local.py' for talker_mtp/make_omni_output in its major findings while its own sweep notes place the same functions in 'modeling_moss_tts_talker.py' — a file-attribution inconsistency that undercuts trust and actionability for two of its five findings. Neither has a LATENT GAP CHECK section, so gap_hit is false for both. X edges out on precision/actionability despite its bad safety claim, since Y's core content is thinner and internally inconsistent.
